view.title: Distress Signal {id}
view.subject: Transmission Subject
view.emailDisabled: Your subspace comm address ({email}) has been disabled. Please contact
view.allowServerAccess: Allow Fleet Command to modify station:
view.ipHelpText: If connection is originating from a different Network Beacon Address, please update it accordingly.
view.passwordEncrypted: Security Clearance Codes are stored in a separate encrypted database.
view.ticketClosed: This distress signal channel is closed, so replies are disabled. If you still require assistance, feel free to broadcast a new distress signal and Fleet Support Command will be happy to assist.
view.replyPlaceholder: Detailed report about the system anomaly
view.replyHint: Creating separate distress signals for new anomalies helps our fleet team prioritize and resolve them more efficiently.
view.userDislikedReply: Crew member disapproved of your transmission
view.closeConfirmText: Do you want to close this distress signal channel?

new.title: Broadcast New Distress Signal
new.subject: Transmission Subject
new.subjectValidation: Please enter a brief description of the system anomaly.
new.descriptionPlaceholder: Starfleet thanks you for your service, Officer! Please submit one distress signal per anomaly to maintain an efficient support system.
new.descriptionValidation: Please enter a detailed description of the system anomaly.
new.fileNote: Note: Only image data files — gif/jpeg/png formats are permitted.
new.allowAccessLabel: I authorize Fleet Command to access my station and perform necessary modifications
new.accessNote: Please note: By broadcasting a support request, Fleet Command may need to access, debug, or modify files in your officer profile. This authorization is required to provide technical support. Please proceed only if you agree.
